criteria_verification.notes: Implemented in turn 1 as `@asynccontextmanager` in `src/forge/adapters/guardkit/progress_subscriber.py`. criteria_verification.criterion_text: `ProgressSink` retains the most recent event per `(build_id,
criteria_verification.evidence: ProgressSink uses `collections.deque(maxlen=N)` for O(1) drop-oldest back-pressure. Verified by `TestProgressSinkRetention::test_back_pressure_drops_oldest` AND by the new BDD scenario `test_edge_case_back_pressure_most_recent_observable` (10 events into a 3-slot sink — Then-step asserts the most-recent 3 are retained and the latest seq=10).
criteria_verification.notes: ProgressSink uses `collections.deque(maxlen=N)` for O(1) drop-oldest back-pressure. Verified by `TestProgressSinkRetention::test_back_pressure_drops_oldest` AND by the new BDD scenario `test_edge_case_back_pressure_most_recent_observable` (10 events into a 3-slot sink — Then-step asserts the most-recent 3 are retained and the latest seq=10).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Unsubscribe runs on context-manager exit, including the exception
criteria_verification.evidence: `async with subscribe_progress` wraps `yield` in try/finally and awaits `sub.unsubscribe()` in the finally; a nested try/except swallows broker-side cleanup errors. Verified by `TestUnsubscribeOnExit::test_unsubscribes_on_normal_exit`, `test_unsubscribes_on_exception_exit`, and `test_unsubscribe_failure_does_not_propagate`. The BDD `test_key_example_progress_streamed_while_running` scenario exercises the normal-exit cleanup path as well.
criteria_verification.notes: `async with subscribe_progress` wraps `yield` in try/finally and awaits `sub.unsubscribe()` in the finally; a nested try/except swallows broker-side cleanup errors. Verified by `TestUnsubscribeOnExit::test_unsubscribes_on_normal_exit`, `test_unsubscribes_on_exception_exit`, and `test_unsubscribe_failure_does_not_propagate`. The BDD `test_key_example_progress_streamed_while_running` scenario exercises the normal-exit cleanup path as well.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: If the NATS client is `None` or unavailable, `subscribe_progress`
criteria_verification.evidence: Two no-op paths: nats_client=None and subscribe() raising. Verified by unit tests `TestUnavailableClient::test_none_client_is_noop_and_warns` and `test_subscribe_failure_yields_noop`, AND by the new BDD scenario `test_edge_case_authoritative_result_when_stream_unavailable` (drives `subscribe_progress(None, ...)` and asserts a `GuardKitResult` is still returned with artefacts AND that the sink carries a `progress_stream_unavailable` warning).
criteria_verification.notes: Two no-op paths: nats_client=None and subscribe() raising. Verified by unit tests `TestUnavailableClient::test_none_client_is_noop_and_warns` and `test_subscribe_failure_yields_noop`, AND by the new BDD scenario `test_edge_case_authoritative_result_when_stream_unavailable` (drives `subscribe_progress(None, ...)` and asserts a `GuardKitResult` is still returned with artefacts AND that the sink carries a `progress_stream_unavailable` warning).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Each subscription is scoped to one
criteria_verification.evidence: `subject_for(build_id, subcommand)` builds the literal `pipeline.stage-complete.{build_id}.{subcommand}` subject; `subscribe_progress` subscribes only to that one subject. Verified by `TestSubjectScoping::test_subscribes_to_correct_subject` and `test_parallel_subscriptions_are_independent` (two `async with` blocks for the same build_id but distinct subcommands receive independent event streams).
criteria_verification.notes: `subject_for(build_id, subcommand)` builds the literal `pipeline.stage-complete.{build_id}.{subcommand}` subject; `subscribe_progress` subscribes only to that one subject. Verified by `TestSubjectScoping::test_subscribes_to_correct_subject` and `test_parallel_subscriptions_are_independent` (two `async with` blocks for the same build_id but distinct subcommands receive independent event streams).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Two concurrent builds against the same repo get isolated sinks (no
criteria_verification.evidence: ProgressSink owns no class- or module-level state — each instance has its own `_buffers` and `_warnings`. Verified by `TestParallelBuilds::test_two_builds_have_isolated_sinks` (two distinct sinks for two build_ids; cross-build queries return None).
criteria_verification.notes: ProgressSink owns no class- or module-level state — each instance has its own `_buffers` and `_warnings`. Verified by `TestParallelBuilds::test_two_builds_have_isolated_sinks` (two distinct sinks for two build_ids; cross-build queries return None).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Invalid payloads (malformed JSON, missing fields) are dropped with a
criteria_verification.evidence: Inbound `_on_envelope` callback wraps `GuardKitProgressEvent.model_validate(envelope.payload)` in try/except for `ValidationError`, `ValueError`, `TypeError`; on failure it records `PROGRESS_PAYLOAD_INVALID` warning and returns. Verified by `TestMalformedPayloads::test_missing_required_field_dropped`, `test_wrong_payload_type_dropped`, and `test_valid_event_after_malformed_still_recorded` (continued consumption after a bad payload).
criteria_verification.notes: Inbound `_on_envelope` callback wraps `GuardKitProgressEvent.model_validate(envelope.payload)` in try/except for `ValidationError`, `ValueError`, `TypeError`; on failure it records `PROGRESS_PAYLOAD_INVALID` warning and returns. Verified by `TestMalformedPayloads::test_missing_required_field_dropped`, `test_wrong_payload_type_dropped`, and `test_valid_event_after_malformed_still_recorded` (continued consumption after a bad payload).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Unit tests with a fake NATS client: ordered delivery, back-pressure
criteria_verification.evidence: 22 unit tests in `test_progress_subscriber.py` using hand-rolled `FakeNATSClient` and `BrokenNATSClient` (no nats-py imports). Coverage matrix: ordered delivery, back-pressure, malformed payload (3 cases), unavailable client (2 paths), parallel subscriptions (2 patterns). Plus 3 BDD scenarios in `tests/bdd/test_guardkit_command_invocation_engine.py` driving the real `subscribe_progress` against a fake NATS client. Total: 25 tests, all passing.
criteria_verification.notes: 22 unit tests in `test_progress_subscriber.py` using hand-rolled `FakeNATSClient` and `BrokenNATSClient` (no nats-py imports). Plus 3 BDD scenarios in `tests/bdd/test_guardkit_command_invocation_engine.py` driving the real `subscribe_progress` against a fake NATS client. Total: 25 tests, all passing.
